creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Trump signs narrower executive order on AI oversight after industry objections
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: After industry objections, President Trump signed a revised AI executive order requiring only voluntary prerelease government reviews of advanced models.
contentSnippet: After industry objections, President Trump signed a revised AI executive order requiring only voluntary prerelease government reviews of advanced models.

title: (No AI) OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work

content: OpenAI is getting serious about courting enterprise users. On Tuesday, the AI lab released a new set of capabilities for Codex, meant to expand the agentic tool’s uses in the workplace. Together with the new tools, the company released an internal report on how Codex is being used for knowledge work, finding its uses go \[…\]

title: (No AI) Microsoft Build 2026: All the news about Windows, AI, RTX Spark and more
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![The Microsoft logo](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/03/STK095_MICROSOFT_2_CVirginia_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Microsoft’s [annual developer conference](https://www.theverge.com/microsoft-build) is kicking off on June 2nd in San Francisco with the [keynote presentation](https://www.theverge.com/tech/941379/microsoft-build-conference-2026-how-to-watch-livestream) streaming [live](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FFMm454fxNA) at 12:30PM ET / 9:30AM PT, and we will be following along here with everything as it’s announced. 

_The Verge_’s Tom Warren [reports](https://www.theverge.com/report/940861/microsoft-build-ai-models-windows-dev-mode-what-to-expect) that we can expect to hear about new AI models and agentic OpenClaw-like tools, plus a [Copilot “super app”](https://www.theverge.com/tech/940058/microsoft-is-reportedly-working-on-its-own-ai-super-app) to go along with some of the [major changes to Windows 11](https://www.theverge.com/news/897834/microsoft-windows-11-quality-performance-commitments-changes) that have already started appearing. Microsoft just announced the new [Surface Laptop Ultra](https://www.theverge.com/tech/940584/microsoft-surface-laptop-ultra-nvidia-rtx-spark-pictures), powered by Nvidia’s RTX Spark, so there could be more Windows on ARM news in store. 

_Follow along here for the latest news and updates._

*   [Microsoft Build 2026 is about to begin.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/941708/microsoft-build-2026-keynote)
*   [How to watch Microsoft’s Build 2026 conference](https://www.theverge.com/tech/941379/microsoft-build-conference-2026-how-to-watch-livestream)
*   [Microsoft to unveil new AI models and Windows improvements at Build](https://www.theverge.com/report/940861/microsoft-build-ai-models-windows-dev-mode-what-to-expect)
*   [Microsoft’s big developer conference returns to San Francisco in June](https://www.theverge.com/news/888004/microsoft-build-2026-san-francisco-june)
contentSnippet:
Microsoft’s annual developer conference is kicking off on June 2nd in San Francisco with the keynote presentation streaming live at 12:30PM ET / 9:30AM PT, and we will be following along here with everything as it’s announced. 
The Verge’s Tom Warren reports that we can expect to hear about new AI models and agentic OpenClaw-like tools, plus a Copilot “super app” to go along with some of the major changes to Windows 11 that have already started appearing. Microsoft just announced the new Surface Laptop Ultra, powered by Nvidia’s RTX Spark, so there could be more Windows on ARM news in store. 

Follow along here for the latest news and updates.

title: (No AI) Gemini Spark is the most impressive and terrifying AI experience I’ve had yet
author: David Pierce
content:
![A slider switching from “Chat” to “Spark”](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/Google-Gemini-Spark.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Spark is Google’s new agentic answer for everything.

According to every product demo from the last four years, planning a trip is a killer use case for AI. Just tell it where you're going, they all promise, and your chatbot / agent / other buzzword will exhaustively search travel options, read up on all the fun things to do, check all the local hotspots, and offer you a fully fledged itinerary. So far, I've found this to work only in the most generic ways: If you want to do the six most obvious things in any city on planet Earth, AI has you covered, but that's about as far as it goes.

I had a very different experience using Spark, [Google's new always-on AI agent](https://www.theverge.com/tech/932996/google-gemini-spark-antigravity-io-2026). Spark is [a hugely ambitious …](https://www.theverge.com/tech/941138/google-gemini-spark-ai-agent-hands-on)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/941388/gemini-spark-ai-agent-trip-planning)

title: (No AI) Travelers deploys AI-powered claims countrywide with OpenAI
content: Travelers built an AI-powered Claim Assistant with OpenAI to guide customers through filing claims, provide 24/7 support, and scale operations during peak demand.
contentSnippet: Travelers built an AI-powered Claim Assistant with OpenAI to guide customers through filing claims, provide 24/7 support, and scale operations during peak demand.

creator: Julian Chokkattu
title: (No AI) Flush With Cash From OpenAI, Opal Is Making an AI-Powered Audio Gadget
dc:creator: Julian Chokkattu
content: Opal, the company famous for making a fancy webcam, has pivoted to making other consumer electronics. Fueled by big investments from OpenAI and Samsung, it’s working on an audio gadget first.
contentSnippet: Opal, the company famous for making a fancy webcam, has pivoted to making other consumer electronics. Fueled by big investments from OpenAI and Samsung, it’s working on an audio gadget first.

creator: Hugo Lowell, Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) The Trump Administration Is at War With Itself Over AI Regulation
dc:creator: Hugo Lowell, Maxwell Zeff
content: Donald Trump killed an executive order to regulate AI. Now, administration officials and AI executives are trying to figure out if there’s anything left to piece back together.
contentSnippet: Donald Trump killed an executive order to regulate AI. Now, administration officials and AI executives are trying to figure out if there’s anything left to piece back together.
